Corn_(maize)___Common_rust.description: Small, cinnamon-brown pustules (raised spots) on both upper and lower leaf surfaces, often arranged in bands or clusters. Thrives in warm (16-28°C), humid conditions.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust.treatment:
1. Plant resistant hybrids like 'Pioneer 30Y87'
2. Apply foliar fungicides (propiconazole or azoxystrobin) at first sign
3. Avoid late planting to escape peak disease season
4. Ensure balanced nitrogen fertilization

Corn_(maize)___healthy.description: Healthy corn leaf with uniform green color, smooth texture, and no visible spots, lesions, or discoloration.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.treatment:
No treatment needed. Maintain good practices:
1. Regular field monitoring
2. Proper irrigation scheduling
3. Balanced NPK fertilization
4. Weed management

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.description: Long, elliptical, cigar-shaped gray-green lesions (2.5-15 cm) that later turn tan/brown. Lesions may merge and blight entire leaves. Caused by Exserohilum turcicum.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment:
1. Plant resistant maize varieties
2. Apply fungicides (azoxystrobin + propiconazole) at early whorl stage
3. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops (soybean, wheat)
4. Remove and destroy crop residue after harvest

Potato___Early_blight.description: Dark brown to black spots with characteristic concentric rings (target pattern) on older leaves first. Yellowing around spots, leading to leaf death. Caused by Alternaria solani.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment:
1. Apply fungicides: mancozeb (2.5g/L) or chlorothalonil at 7-10 day intervals
2. Remove and destroy infected lower leaves
3. Maintain proper plant spacing for airflow
4. Apply balanced fertilizer (avoid nitrogen deficiency)

Potato___healthy.description: Healthy potato leaf with uniform green color, no spots or lesions, normal texture. Plant shows vigorous growth.
Potato___healthy.treatment:
No treatment required. Prevention tips:
1. Use certified disease-free seed potatoes
2. Practice 3-year crop rotation
3. Proper hilling and irrigation management
4. Regular field scouting every 7 days

Potato___Late_blight.description: Irregular water-soaked pale green spots that rapidly enlarge in cool (10-22°C), wet weather. White cottony mold on leaf undersides during humid conditions. Caused by Phytophthora infestans.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment:
1. Apply systemic fungicides: metalaxyl + mancozeb immediately
2. Use protective sprays before disease appears in susceptible regions
3. Destroy infected plants (burn/bury deep)
4. Ensure good field drainage
5. Avoid overhead irrigation

Tomato___Early_blight.description: Dark brown to black spots with concentric rings (target pattern) starting on older, lower leaves. Leaves yellow, wither, and drop. Can also affect stems and fruit. Caused by Alternaria solani.
Tomato___Early_blight.treatment:
1. Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicides at 7-14 day intervals
2. Remove infected lower leaves immediately
3. Mulch around plants to reduce soil splash
4. Stake plants for better air circulation
5. Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 2-3 years

Tomato___healthy.description: Healthy tomato leaf with vibrant green color, normal shape, no spots, lesions, or yellowing. Plant is disease-free and thriving.
Tomato___healthy.treatment:
No treatment needed. Maintain health with:
1. Regular drip irrigation (avoid wetting foliage)
2. Balanced NPK fertilization (15-15-15)
3. Proper spacing (60-90 cm between plants)
4. Weekly field monitoring for early detection

Tomato___Late_blight.description: Pale green, irregular water-soaked spots on leaves that rapidly enlarge and turn brown/black. White fuzzy mold appears on undersides in high humidity. Can destroy entire field in 7-10 days. Caused by Phytophthora infestans.
Tomato___Late_blight.treatment:
1. Apply systemic fungicide IMMEDIATELY: metalaxyl + mancozeb or cymoxanil + mancozeb
2. Spray every 5-7 days during wet periods
3. Remove and destroy all infected plants
4. Improve greenhouse/field ventilation
5. Use drip irrigation instead of overhead watering
6. Plant resistant varieties if available

Unknown.description: No information available for this disease class.
Unknown.treatment: Please consult an agricultural expert or your local extension office for proper diagnosis and treatment recommendations.
